name: Cleveland Church Of Christ
description_short: Federal credit union established in 1975 serving members of religious groups in Cleveland, Ohio with savings accounts, auto loans, and personal banking services.
description_long: Cleveland Church of Christ Federal Credit Union was established in 1975 and is headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative dedicated to serving its community. The institution has grown to serve over 400 loyal members through a single branch location at 1035 E 105th Street in Cleveland. The credit union offers a comprehensive range of personal financial services including savings accounts (Regular Savings, Share Draft, Money Market, and Share Certificates/CDs), auto loans for both new and used vehicles, personal loans, wire transfers, and online banking with mobile app access. Members can also access financial counseling, financial education resources, and various calculators to support their financial planning. The institution emphasizes competitive rates and lower fees as a not-for-profit entity, offering 0% APR on new vehicle loans, used vehicle loans, and unsecured credit cards, with other unsecured loans at 7.60%. What distinguishes Cleveland Church of Christ FCU is its specialized focus on serving members of religious groups, aligning financial services with spiritual values and community needs. The credit union operates within a faith-based framework, providing services tailored to members of the Cleveland Church of Christ community. This niche focus creates a tight-knit membership base with shared values and mutual financial support principles typical of credit unions. However, potential members should be aware of current operational challenges. The website indicates the lobby is currently closed and the institution may be experiencing service issues. The credit union operates with minimal infrastructure—only 1 branch and 0 ATMs—which significantly limits accessibility for members. Additionally, membership eligibility is restricted to members of religious groups, specifically those affiliated with Cleveland Church of Christ, making it unavailable to the general public. The small size (0 employees listed as of 2026, though this appears to be a data error) and limited branch network may restrict service availability and convenience compared to larger financial institutions.
pros: 0% APR on new vehicle loans — significantly lower than market rates
0% APR on used vehicle loans and unsecured credit cards — competitive rates for members
Not-for-profit structure typically results in better rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Over 400 members with established community and shared values
Mobile app available for iOS and Android with online banking access
Established since 1975 — nearly 50 years of operational history
Offers financial counseling and financial education services
cons: Lobby currently closed — limited in-person service availability
Only 1 branch and 0 ATMs — severely limited physical access and convenience
May be experiencing service issues per website notice — potential reliability concerns
Restricted membership to religious group members only — not available to general public
Very small institution with $383,100 in assets — limited resources and financial stability compared to larger institutions
best_for: Members of Cleveland Church of Christ seeking basic savings and loan services within their faith community
Auto buyers affiliated with the church looking for 0% APR vehicle financing
Individuals in Cleveland, Ohio seeking credit union membership with faith-based alignment
diagnosis: Cleveland Church of Christ Federal Credit Union is best for members of the Cleveland Church of Christ community seeking basic personal banking, savings, and auto financing with competitive rates. The primary caveat is severely limited accessibility due to closure of the lobby, only one physical branch location, zero ATMs, ongoing service issues, and restricted membership eligibility to religious group members only.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
